name: Repo-wide speed + efficiency: build and research pipeline

description: Baseline session setup: added auto/program.md, auto/autoresearch.sh, and autoresearch.checks.sh; benchmarking pnpm build as primary metric.
Memoized getPieces() and getPieceFragments() in lib/pieces.ts so static generation, metadata, and OG routes reuse parsed content instead of rereading all markdown files.

name: Repo-wide speed + efficiency: cold pnpm build

description: Reset session to cold-build benchmarking by clearing .next in auto/autoresearch.sh and updated auto/program.md; established corrected baseline with memoized content loading still in place.
Enabled Next experimental optimizePackageImports for lucide-react to reduce module processing during cold production builds.
Tried removing the redundant Google Fonts CSS @import from app/globals.css, but cold build time regressed materially under the benchmark.
Tried excluding __tests__ directories from tsconfig.json so Next build would scan less TypeScript, but cold build time regressed under the benchmark.
Validation run: reverted lucide-react optimizePackageImports to test whether the prior win was noise; cold build regressed, so keeping the lucide import optimization.
Tried targeted piece helpers (getPieceSlugs/getPieceBySlug) so static params and OG metadata could avoid full collection loads, but cold build was still slower than the cached all-pieces approach.
Validation run: removed lib/pieces.ts memoization to measure its cold-build impact directly; build got slightly slower, so keep the cached content-loader path.
Resumed session and tried a lighter PieceSummary path for static params/metadata/OG routes, but duplicating summary loads alongside full getPieces() made cold builds much worse.
Control re-run on the current kept code to measure cold-build noise; no code changes, result used only to improve confidence calibration.
Removed four unused Geist pixel font variants from app/layout.tsx and app/globals.css, keeping only the mono pixel font that is actually referenced.
Confirmation run on the unchanged font-cleanup HEAD; result bounced back above baseline, so the improvement is likely small and noisy even though the cleanup itself is harmless.
Expanded optimizePackageImports to more client libraries, but the result was effectively baseline-level and clearly worse than the current best kept build.
Tried isolating a cheap getPieceSlugs() helper just for generateStaticParams(), but even that split path regressed compared with the existing cached getPieces() approach.
Tried removing TypeScript incremental mode from tsconfig.json for cold builds, but the build got slower.

Tried next/cache unstable_cache for getPieces(); benchmark looked promising, but correctness checks failed because unstable_cache requires Next incremental cache context and breaks Vitest/script usage.
Made unstable_cache safe for tests/scripts with a direct-load fallback; checks passed and build improved over baseline, but it was still worse than the current best kept result.
Tried deferring @huggingface/inference in lib/retrieval.ts with a dynamic import to shrink build-time server module work, but cold build time regressed.
Tried lazy-loading AI route dependencies inside app/api/chat/route.ts so build would process less server code up front, but cold build time regressed sharply.
Tried synchronous readFileSync in lib/pieces.ts to cut async overhead, but it made cold builds much slower.
Tried moving large tooltip dictionaries to JSON, but the build crashed during prerender because the JSON wrapper did not match the runtime import shape.
Removed next/font/google usage from app/layout.tsx and relied on the existing Google Fonts CSS import in app/globals.css, avoiding duplicate font-loading/build work.
Confirmation re-run on the unchanged font-simplified HEAD; result stayed close to the previous win, increasing confidence without introducing a new code change.

name: Research pipeline + build speed: deterministic daily research benchmark

description: New session baseline: deterministic local research-pipeline benchmark with a mock autoresearch repo/command, plus monitored embedding prep and cold build metrics.
Cached autoresearch dependency setup in scripts/research/autoresearch.ts so a single daily run no longer refetches/recheckouts the dependency once per piece.
Control re-run on the unchanged repo-setup cache HEAD; research benchmark stayed essentially identical, increasing confidence in the large win.
Made research output URL collection iterative and parallelized file reads within each directory in scripts/research/autoresearch.ts.
Suppressed git setup command stdio during autoresearch dependency sync, but the research benchmark regressed versus the current best.
Replaced query construction with a bounded imperative loop in gatherAutoResearchResults(), but it remained slower than the current best.
Cached the output date label once per process in scripts/research/autoresearch.ts; it helped a little but still did not beat the current best.
Replaced URL parsing in buildSearchResults() with simple hostname regex extraction, but the benchmark regressed to the slower range.
Skipped unconditional outputDir mkdir for custom command runs in gatherAutoResearchResults(), but the benchmark still did not beat the current best.
Switched parseDate() to Date.parse() on the shared piece-loading path; it helped a little but still did not beat the current best research benchmark.
Added an includes() fast-path before update-marker regex scanning in lib/pieces.ts, but it still underperformed the current best research run.
Replaced parseFrontmatter() with a direct line scanner to reduce allocations on piece loading, but it regressed the research benchmark.
Control re-run on the unchanged current best research HEAD; result stayed in the same range and confirms the provider/setup wins remain stable.
